title: Part Of A Ghazal
content:
Lonely rose out-splendouring legions of roses,
    How could the nightingales behold you and not sing?

    By Rustwell of Georgia (from the Tariel, twelfth century).
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Powys Mathers (As Translator), titled "Part Of A Ghazal", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
